items.q: What was the theory of the four humours?
items.correct: Disease was caused by imbalance of blood, phlegm, yellow bile and black bile; treatment aimed to rebalance them via bloodletting, purging or diet. It dominated for nearly 2,000 years.
items.misconception: Medieval doctors knew about germs but used humours because they had no medicine.
items.random_wrong: The four humours were invented by Pasteur to explain bacteria.
items.why: Humoral theory was a coherent system from Hippocrates and Galen, not ignorance. Students retroject germ theory backwards, missing why educated people believed it for so long.

items.q: How did medieval people explain the Black Death?
items.correct: Most blamed God's punishment for sin or bad air (miasma); some scapegoated Jews; doctors blamed planetary alignments and imbalanced humours.
items.misconception: Medieval people knew it was spread by rats and fleas but had no way to stop it.
items.random_wrong: Medieval people thought it was caused by germs they couldn't see.
items.why: Germ theory did not exist until the 19th century. Students retroject modern knowledge onto medieval responses, missing the religious and humoral frameworks that shaped real behaviour.

items.q: Why was Vesalius significant in the history of medicine?
items.correct: His 1543 De Humani Corporis Fabrica corrected Galen's errors via human dissection and accurate illustration, founding modern anatomy though not curing disease.
items.misconception: Vesalius discovered the cause of disease and ended humoral theory.
items.why: Vesalius improved anatomy but disease theory still ran on humours for centuries. Students conflate progress in one area (structure) with progress in another (cause of illness).

items.q: Which doctor's ideas dominated Western medicine for around 1,500 years?
items.why: Correct answer: Galen. “Hippocrates” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Roughly what fraction of England's population died in the Black Death of 1348-49? Enter as a percentage.
items.why: Exact answer: 33. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
